summary: BUG CONFIRMED: Item Quick Create - Form closes after saving new master data (Category/Unit/Brand/Supplier). Expected behavior: Item Form should remain open with new master auto-selected.

ui_bugs.issue: After saving new master data via Quick Create (+ Tambah Kategori/Satuan/Merek/Supplier), the Item Form modal closes instead of staying open
steps_to_reproduce: 6. BUG: Item Form modal closes, user is back to Items list
ui_bugs.expected_behavior: Item Form should remain open with the newly created category auto-selected in the field
ui_bugs.actual_behavior: Item Form modal closes after quick create save, user loses all other form data they may have entered

issues: NESTED FORM HTML VIOLATION: QuickCreateModal uses <form> inside ItemFormModal's <form>. This violates HTML spec and can cause unpredictable browser behavior
Console warning: 'In HTML, <form> cannot be a descendant of <form>. This will cause a hydration error.'

action_items: FIX REQUIRED: Prevent Item Form from closing when Quick Create saves
ROOT CAUSE: Nested <form> elements - QuickCreateModal.jsx line 145 creates <form> inside ItemFormModal.jsx line 466's <form>
SOLUTION 1: Change QuickCreateModal to use <div> instead of <form> and handle submit via button onClick
SOLUTION 2: Add e.stopPropagation() to QuickCreateModal's handleSubmit
SOLUTION 3: Move QuickCreateModal outside of ItemFormModal using React Portal
VERIFY: After fix, ensure new master data is auto-selected in the dropdown after save
critical_code_review_comments: QuickCreateModal.jsx line 145: Uses <form> element which gets nested inside ItemFormModal's form
QuickCreateModal.jsx line 117: Calls onClose() after onSuccess(), causing double state updates
SearchableSelectWithCreate line 369-370: setShowQuickCreate(false) and setIsOpen(false) are called in handleQuickCreateSuccess, but QuickCreateModal also calls onClose() which is redundant
ItemFormModal.jsx line 466: Parent <form> element that contains the nested QuickCreateModal
MasterItems.jsx: Does not pass onRefreshMasterData callback to ItemFormModal
seed_data_creation: Created test category TEST-KAT-9930 during bug reproduction
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug confirmed in Item Quick Create. After main agent fixes the nested form issue, need to retest all quick create buttons: Category, Unit, Brand, Supplier. Also verify auto-selection of new item in dropdown after save.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: HTML spec violation - nested <form> elements causing browser to mishandle form submission
rca_of_the_issue.technical_detail: QuickCreateModal uses <form> element (line 145) which gets rendered inside ItemFormModal's <form> (line 466). When QuickCreateModal submits, even with e.preventDefault(), the browser behavior is unpredictable due to nested forms. The parent form (ItemFormModal) appears to be affected by the child form's submission event.
evidence: Console warning: 'In HTML, <form> cannot be a descendant of <form>'
Screenshots show Item Form closes immediately after Quick Create save
Test output: '❌ BUG CONFIRMED: Item Form CLOSED after quick create!'
rca_of_the_issue.recommended_fix: Replace <form> with <div> in QuickCreateModal and trigger submission via button onClick with type='button'. This eliminates the nested form issue while maintaining the same functionality.
